notes: Shell-escape command for Gemini CLI — asserts the CORRECT arc the bug fix delivers (was known_failing pre-#660). A warmup turn establishes the session first (Gemini only persists a shell-escape once the session exists). A `!echo hello` shell-escape then runs in a LOCAL shell with NO LLM round-trip (handleShellCommand → ShellExecutionService.execute, never chat.sendMessage), but Gemini persists it to the session rollout IMMEDIATELY as an ordinary `type:"user"` text message ('I ran the following shell command: ...') with NO following `gemini` turn. parseUser must SKIP that preamble (mirroring claudecode's isLocalCommandContent skip of <bash-input>/<bash-stdout>) so state STAYS ready through shell_escape_window; without the fix it drives ready→working with no settle and STICKS in working (Gemini emits no terminal `gemini` text turn for a shell-escape). A final normal turn proves the session is still alive and a real prompt still drives ready→working→ready. Phases carry no same_session_as: a transient proc-<PID> presession is ready first and would pin session_birth to it, so turn phases match the real session by event order (as in basic-turn). Observations assert the websocket metric vector: cost and tokens non-zero (the warmup + final turns bill input/output) and agent gemini-cli; model is left unpinned because the live default is account-dependent.

text: Session appears in ready shortly after gemini -y launches
Launcher PID bound via cwd from the bootstrap <session_context> (heap-bump worker excluded)
A warmup prompt drives ready→working, establishing the session so the later shell-escape is persisted to its rollout
The warmup 'ready' gemini text turn settles working→ready — the session is now established and idle

After the !echo shell-escape runs locally (no LLM round-trip) and is persisted as a bare user preamble, state must STAY ready — the parser skips the shell-escape preamble (mirroring claudecode's <bash-input>/<bash-stdout> skip). KNOWN-FAILING without the fix: the parser instead drives ready→working with no settle, sticking in working.
After the shell-escape window, a normal user prompt drives ready→working — proves the session is still alive and a real prompt still opens a turn
The terminal 'alive' gemini text turn (non-empty content, zero toolCalls → turn_done) settles working→ready; state never entered waiting
REPL torn down after the final turn → a single process_exited on the session; state immediately before teardown is ready
